exemplar.title: Evaluating the Accuracy of Finite Difference Approximations for Solving Differential Equations using a Pharmacokinetic Model for the Elimination of Ibuprofen

detailedExemplarData.title: Evaluating the Accuracy of Finite Difference Approximations for Solving Differential Equations using a Pharmacokinetic Model for the Elimination of Ibuprofen

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: The exploration is highly coherent with a smooth narrative progression through data selection, modelling, numerical schemes and error analysis.
Well-structured with a clear contents page, numbered sections, headings and a concise conclusion facilitating easy navigation.
weaknesses: Several lengthy pharmacological background sections dilute conciseness and could be condensed.
Minor formatting inconsistencies (e.g. dot leaders in the contents page, citation style) slightly detract from polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands are assessed at band 4 (coherence and organization) and one at band 3 (conciseness). The work demonstrates exceptional logical development and organization, meriting the maximum mark, with only minor issues in conciseness and formatting preventing it from being âhighly conciseâ.

strengths: Mostly consistent and accurate use of mathematical language and notation (calculus symbols, subscripts, Taylor series) throughout the text.
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, algebraic derivations, schematic diagrams) to support understanding.
Logical explanations and step-by-step derivations are generally clear for an HL audience.
weaknesses: A few graphs lack clear axis labels/units and resolution could be improved.
Occasional overuse of math mode in headings and missing uncertainty measures in regression results reduce clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall into band 3: notation is mostly consistent; representations are effective but imperfect; structure is good but could be streamlined. These justify a mid-level mark of 3.

strengths: Clear personal motivation linking pharmacology and mathematics shows genuine engagement.
Choice of a Hoerl decay model and a self-devised comparative error study demonstrate significant initiative.
Integration of chemistry knowledge (e.g. linearization via ln C) reflects independent thinking.
weaknesses: Methods remain within standard HL scope and do not reach an innovative threshold.
Extension ideas (e.g. testing on independent datasets) are suggested but not pursued.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach and good creativity, placing it solidly in band 2. While engaging, it lacks the exceptional innovation required for the top mark.

strengths: Thoughtful analysis of error orders and discussion of inconsistencies between theoretical and empirical results.
Clear evaluation of methodological strengths, weaknesses, rounding effects and coding improvements.
weaknesses: Reflection is integrated but does not probe deeply into future implications or broader methodological critiques.
Opportunities to quantify the impact of rounding errors or spreadsheet round-off are noted but not fully explored.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection is meaningful throughout (band 2), with thoughtful analysis and clear outcome evaluation. However, it stops short of the deep, comprehensive critical reflection needed for band 3.

strengths: Comprehensive and expert use of relevant mathematics: ODEs, exponential modelling, multiple finite-difference schemes and Taylor series error analysis.
Techniques are fully commensurate with and at times exceed HL syllabus expectations, applied coherently to the problem.
Derivations and computations demonstrate thorough understanding with only minor numerical or transcription slips.
weaknesses: Minor inaccuracies (e.g. oscillations in central scheme) are noted without full stability analysis.
Error tables could be enhanced with percentage error columns and deeper discussion of higher-order derivative impacts.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are assessed in the 5-mark band: mathematics is comprehensive, fully at course level and mostly correct. Minor omissions prevent the expert, flawless work required for the top 6-mark band, thus a mark of 5 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: **Logical structure and clarity of explanations** â Derivations are shown step-by-step (e.g., separation of variables, finite-difference rearrangements).  Arguments are mostly clear to a reader with HL background.  A few bulky algebraic passages could be streamlined, but overall communication is âgoodâ rather than exemplary, justifying band 3.

**Understanding and accuracy** â Calculations are mostly correct; derivations of error orders, ke estimation and regression use appropriate reasoning.  Minor numerical slips (oscillatory central scheme values, transcription inaccuracies) are acknowledged but do not undermine core understanding.  This corresponds to âthorough understanding and mostly correctâ, i.e., the 5-mark band.

**Multiple representations** â The student employs tables of raw and computed values, graphs (scatter, regression, semi-log), algebraic derivations and schematic diagrams.  These representations are well chosen and support understanding, fulfilling the âeffective useâ criterion in band 3; a few graphs are low-resolution or only partially labelled, so the highest band is not fully met.

**Coherence and logical development** â The studentâs narrative moves smoothly from data selection, through modelling, to successive numerical techniques and error analysis.  Each major idea (analytical model â three finite-difference schemes â Taylor-based error study) follows logically and is explicitly sign-posted.  Occasional digressions (e.g. pharmacology background) do not break the overall chain of reasoning.  This sustained, well-sequenced progression is characteristic of the top band.

**Creativity and initiative** â Employing three finite-difference variants, designing a comparative error study and using spreadsheet automation demonstrate a good level of initiative.  Methods, however, are well-known and the approach is not truly innovative, giving a solid band 2 score.

**Critical analysis** â Error orders are derived and compared, results are interpreted, and inconsistencies (e.g., RÂ² vs. theoretical error) are discussed.  Analysis is thoughtful but not âdeep and insightfulâ across the whole piece, meeting band 2.

**Evaluation of outcomes** â The conclusion identifies methodological strengths/weaknesses, rounding effects and potential coding improvements, demonstrating a clear, if not comprehensive, evaluation.  Band 2 is therefore appropriate.

**Depth of reflection** â The student reflects on modelling choices, step-size effects, computational efficiency and study limitations throughout.  Reflection is integrated but not probing to a critical depth; hence âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ (band 2) is the best fit.

**Mathematical language and notation** â Correct use of calculus symbols, subscripts, Ît, ke, Taylor series notation and units is evident almost everywhere.  Minor inconsistencies (mixing plain text and math mode, occasional absent units) prevent the description from being flawless, so âmostly consistentâ (band 3) is appropriate.

**Personal approach** â The narrative links the studentâs interest in pharmacology and chemistry to the mathematical investigation, and decisions (e.g., step sizes, modelling choices) are explained from a personal perspective.  This clear personal stamp places the work in band 2.

**Conciseness and relevance** â While the majority of content is pertinent, several lengthy narrative sections (e.g. detailed pharmacological back-story, extended screenshot appendices) add bulk without advancing the mathematics.  At ~20 pages, the exploration is not âhighly conciseâ, yet most material remains relevant.  This aligns with band 3: âconcise with mostly relevant contentâ.

**Relevance of mathematics** â Differential equations, exponential modelling, three finite-difference schemes, Taylor series and global error formulae are all directly relevant to the stated aim and applied throughout the paper.  The mathematics is both broad and consistently connected to the problem, fitting the âcomprehensiveâ (5-mark) descriptor.

**Independent thinking** â Choice of a pharmacokinetic data set, derivation of backward/central schemes from first principles and self-devised error formulae show more than routine application.  Nevertheless, techniques remain within standard HL scope and do not reach the âoutstandingâ threshold, so band 2 reflects âsignificant evidenceâ.

**Organization and structure** â A contents page, numbered sections, clear headings (Introduction, Analytical Solution, Finite Difference Approximations, etc.) and a final conclusion give the work a textbook-like structure.  Paragraphing is tidy and diagrams/tables are embedded at appropriate points.  Readers can effortlessly locate information, satisfying the descriptor for an âexceptionally well organisedâ exploration.

**Level appropriateness** â All techniques (ODE solution, finite-difference derivations, Taylor-based error analysis) sit squarely at or slightly above HL syllabus expectations, and are applied purposefully.  The work merits the âfully commensurate and well-appliedâ 5-mark band.

annotations.comment: The title clearly states the investigationâs focus and is concise, guiding the reader immediately to the mathematical and pharmacokinetic context.
The contents page is well structured, but the dot leaders are inconsistent and could be formatted more neatly to improve readability.
The table of contents effectively outlines sections, but math mode is overused in headings. Reserve LaTeX for actual formulae to avoid clutter.
The introduction explores personal interest and HL calculus well, but the lengthy pharmacology background could be condensed to maintain relevance.
The student connects pharmacology and mathematics through personal motivation, demonstrating genuine engagement with the topic.
When defining pharmacokinetic terms (e.g., AUC, Câââ), include brief definitions to aid clarity for readers less familiar with pharmacology.
Figure 1 is well placed, but axis labels and units are missing; add clear axis titles to enhance mathematical communication.
The student critically evaluates data source limitations and discusses internal validity â a strong reflection on methodological implications.
Table 1 is clear and concise; rounding to three significant figures is explained, but the impact on analytical error could be quantified explicitly.
Embedding the Desmos graph supports multiple representations effectively; consider ensuring all graphs have gridlines and axis ticks for precision.
The choice of a Hoerl model shows initiative in seeking a curve that decays to zero; linking the RÂ² improvement to model properties is effective.
When presenting regression parameters (a=77.2, b=0.516, c=0.638), include standard errors or confidence bounds to support understanding of parameter uncertainty.
The derivation of the analytical solution is thorough; steps are clear and the separation of variables is correctly applied.
The integration constant c is introduced but not explicitly eliminated; clarify why c=0 in the solution for complete logical clarity.
The linearization via ln C was well motivated by chemistry knowledge, demonstrating integrated thinking across disciplines.
When plotting ln C vs t, specify units on axes and include the regression equation on the graph for full mathematical communication.
The final analytical model C=39.4e^{-0.356t} fits remarkably well, but consider testing with an independent dataset to validate generalizability.
Introduction to finite differences is clear; consider summarizing the order of accuracy of each scheme upfront to guide the reader.
Eulerâs forward difference derivation correctly connects to first principles; include a brief note on its local and global error orders here.
The forward finite difference table is well presented; a graph overlaying the numerical and analytical solutions would enhance comparison.
The backward scheme derivation from first principles is thorough; demonstrating the implicit nature of the method deepens understanding.
When rearranging for C(t), clarify the handling of the implicit equation in numerical implementation to aid reproducibility.
The central difference derivation effectively cancels terms to reveal a second-order formula; this shows strong mathematical communication.
The oscillatory nature of the central scheme is noted but not fully discussed; consider adding a brief stability analysis.
Table 8 neatly summarizes kâ errors; adding percentage error columns would provide additional insight into relative accuracy.
Defining global error via GTE=LTEÂ·X/h is well motivated; note also that higher derivatives vary with C(t), impacting theoretical error.
The Taylor expansion to derive LTE is rigorous and demonstrates deep understanding of error orders.
Including local error expressions for all three methods allows clear comparison; consider summarizing them in a single comparative table.
The calculated global errors demonstrate scheme efficiency well; discuss how round-off error from spreadsheets might affect the numeric values.
The conclusion offers reflective insight on limitations and skills gained; propose specific extensions (e.g., stiff ODEs) to deepen future investigations.
References are consistently formatted and relevant; consider using a consistent citation style (e.g., APA) for full presentation coherence.
